investigation: Anthropic API cache write cost — $83 in 6 days

system_prompt_sizes.verdict: System prompts are NOT the problem. All under 1700 tokens.

reporter/mesh_asia_growth_prospects.md.risk: HIGH — if loaded as context on every call

reporter/country_research_matrix.md.risk: HIGH — if loaded as context on every call

root_cause_analysis.primary_suspect: Conversation history length — 22M cache write tokens in 6 days is NOT explained by system prompts alone (max ~10K tokens). The most likely cause is long conversation threads being re-sent to the API on every message. Each Telegram message to an agent replays the full session history.
root_cause_analysis.secondary_suspect: Reporter agent's large workspace files (country_research_matrix.md at 78K chars, mesh_asia_growth_prospects.md at 96K chars) if OpenClaw is auto-loading all workspace files as context.
root_cause_analysis.calculation: 22M tokens / 6 days = 3.67M tokens/day. At ~10K tokens per agent call, this implies ~367 agent API calls per day — far more than manual interactions explain. Scheduled heartbeats, cron-triggered agent calls, and morning briefings all fire daily and each replays conversation history.
biggest_cost_drivers: 1. Morning briefing cron (8am) fires TWICE daily — growth + starter — each replaying full conversation history
2. Afternoon pipeline check (5pm) fires TWICE — growth + starter
3. Evening improvement report (8pm) fires THREE times — growth + starter + reporter
5. Each cron call replays full session history which grows longer every day

recommendations: 1. Switch all cron-triggered agent calls to isolated sessions (no history replay) — each should be stateless
2. Archive or move large reporter workspace files out of auto-loaded context
3. Reduce morning/afternoon/evening briefing frequency or consolidate into one daily call
4. Consider switching scheduled cron agents to a cheaper model (haiku) for routine checks
5. Do NOT load improvements_*.md, outreach_drafts_*.md, pipeline_status_*.md into context automatically
